opportunities.description: Weed n' Mulch at South Street Urban Farm Office of Sustainability, Resilience and Future Ready 0 similar opportunities Similar Opportunities Description Location: 626 W South St. Orlando, FL 32805 Time: 8:00 am - 10:00 am Parking: Parking will be available on the street next to the garden and in the grass lot across the street, located at 617 W South Street Orlando, FL 32805. Weekly summer maintenance at South Street Urban Farm, including weeding, mulching, planting, and garden management. Please bring water, sunscreen and closed toe shoes! Bathrooms are not available on-site, so please plan accordingly. --------------------------------------------- The Office of Sustainability, Resilience and Future Ready is thrilled to offer Volunteer Workdays at South Street Urban Farm! opportunities.title: Composting at South Street Urban Farm
opportunities.description: Composting at South Street Urban Farm Office of Sustainability, Resilience and Future Ready 0 similar opportunities Similar Opportunities Description Location: 626 W South St. Orlando, FL 32805 Time: 10:30 am - 12:00 pm Parking: Parking will be available on the street next to the garden and in the grass lot across the street, located at 617 W South Street Orlando, FL 32805. Join us on Mondays and/or Thursdays at South Street Urban Farm to get your hands dirty, learn new skills, and help build a more sustainable community. Each week, we''ll be processing food scraps collected from a local community organization. Volunteers will help chop food waste, mix it with mulch, and load it into our onsite composter. These efforts directly divert organic material from the landfill and help create nutrient-rich compost that supports the health of our gardens. Please bring water, sunscreen and closed toe shoes! Bathrooms are not available on-site, so please plan accordingly. --------------------------------------------- The Office of Sustainability, Resilience and Future Ready is thrilled to offer Volunteer Workdays at South Street Urban Farm! opportunities.title: Red, White & Woof
opportunities.description: Red, White & Woof The Mennello Museum of American Art 0 similar opportunities Similar Opportunities Description Volunteers needed on July 11th, 2026 from 10:00am to 2pm. Celebrate National All-American Pet Photo Day, a holiday dedicated to capturing and sharing photos of everyone''s beloved pets, with a festive day at the Mennello Museum of American Art! Join us for the setup and takedown of Red, White & Woof, a patriotic, pet-friendly celebration from 11AM to 1PM filled with art-inspired activities throughout our lakeside Sculpture Garden. Enjoy themed photo vignettes, a star-spangled pet costume contest, local vendors, treats (including pet-friendly options!), and interactive fun for the whole family. Volunteers will assist Museum staff with set up and break down, welcoming guests, and supervising safe play with musuem supplies. Volunteers are expected to stay during the entire duration of the event. Volunteers are needed to stay the entire duration of their shift, please only sign up if you can commit to the full 4 hours. Please feel free to bring water and snacks. We will be working outside of the museum. Please dress appropriately for the hot weather. Dress Code: Comfortable walking shoes, long pants, and a full shirt (no crop or tank tops) with no inappropriate subject matter. Additional info: Minimum age: 16 Availability Schedule Shifts Opportunity available: 06/23/2026 through 07/11/2026 Only 4 Positions available! There are no available shifts for this opportunity. S

opportunities.title: Plastic Free July Cleanup
opportunities.description: Plastic Free July Cleanup Keep Orlando Beautiful 0 similar opportunities Similar Opportunities Description Join Keep Orlando Beautiful for a community cleanup! All month long we are celebrating Plastic Free July. This means we are ditching the single-use plastics and choosing reusables! This cleanup we will be focusing on removing these single-use plastics like water bottles, utensils, and straws from our beautiful wild spaces. Eagle Nest Park Saturday, July 18 8:00 - 10:00 a.m. Please dress for a mess as volunteers will be working outdoors. Please wear close-toed shoes and bring other items such as sun-screen, bug spray, a hat or other items as needed. Make sure to bring your reusbale water bottle and help us go plastic free! We will have a water cooler with a spout for refills. If you forget your water bottle water fountains are also available. We hope to see you at the event! Additional info: Location Where the opportunity will occur 5165 Metrowest Blvd Orlando , FL 32811 Location Description: Sign in at the tables near the restroom. Get Directions Coordinates: 28.5171,-81.449721 Contact Info Contact person for the opportunity Contact 1 Email: keeporlandobeautiful@orlando.gov Primary Phone: (407) 246-2752 Contact 2 Contact Address Address to use when contacting someone about this opportunity. opportunities.title: Strawless Summer Restaurant Crawl
opportunities.description: Strawless Summer Restaurant Crawl Keep Orlando Beautiful 0 similar opportunities Similar Opportunities Description Strawless Summer is an Orlando-wide initiative led by the Surfrider Orlando Chapter and Keep Orlando Beautiful to help local restaurants reduce single-use plastic waste! For 2 months (July through August 2026), participating businesses pledge to only provide straws upon customer request. This simple shift helps: Cut down on unnecessary single-use waste Save restaurants money on straw costs Prevent straw debris from littering Orlando We need volunteers to help us invite restaurants to join us! Monday June 22 - Milk District Wednesday June 24 - Ivanhoe Village Thursday June 25 - Mills 50 Check volunteer shift for exact locations. Volunteer Activities: Join the crawl and break into small groups. Each group will visit several restaurants sharing the Strawless Summer flyer. Help restaurants sign up online. Pass out buttons and signage if they sign up! Join us in making a positive impact and support a cleaner Orlando! opportunities.title: Free Family Funday - JULY 2026
opportunities.description: Free Family Funday - JULY 2026 The Mennello Museum of American Art 0 similar opportunities Similar Opportunities Description Volunteers needed on July 12th, 2026 from 11:00am to 4:30pm. On the 2nd Sunday of every month, the Mennello Museum of American Art has free admission all day, plus a fine art project! Each Free Family Funday has a different art project based on the artists of the current exhibition, their subject matter, and their methods. Volunteers will assist Museum staff with set up and break down, explaining and helping children and families with art projects, and keeping a clean space. Volunteers are expected to stay during the entire duration of the event. Volunteers are needed to stay the entire duration of their shift, please only sign up if you can commit to the full 5.5 hours. Please feel free to bring water and snacks. We will be working outside of the museum either by the lake in the shade or on the front patio. Dress Code: Comfortable walking shoes, long pants, and a full shirt (no crop or tank tops) with no inappropriate subject matter. opportunities.title: Volunteer: Orlando Wetlands Visitor Center
opportunities.description: Volunteer: Orlando Wetlands Visitor Center Orlando Wetlands 0 similar opportunities Similar Opportunities Description Join our community of friendly & dedicated volunteers, the Friends of the Orlando Wetlands, to support the mission and operations of the Orlando Wetlands. Become an Orlando Wetlands ambassador today! BEFORE SIGNING UP: Attending an orientation session is required. Upcoming 2026 options: Sat, July 25th Sat, September 19th Sat, November 14th 8:30 AM - 11:30 AM. Times/dates subject to change. LIMITED SPOTS, RSVP REQUIRED. Activities include: Front Desk: Tues - Sat General operation of the Visitor Center, including opening/closing procedures, greet visitors, provide info, phone reception, restock, light cleaning/organization, process transactions, operate basic tech, & other duties as needed/assigned. Docent: Tues - Sat Greet visitors, interpret displays, live animals & models, lead hands-on interactive activities, run the Education Cart, operate basic tech, interact with all ages, stand/sit for long periods of time, & other duties as needed/assigned. Time Commitment: Volunteers participate on an ongoing & recurring basis such as weekly or monthly. Shifts ~ 4 hours in length; 8:30 AM - 12:30 PM (morning) or 12:15 PM - 4:30 PM (afternoon). Setting: Most work is indoors/directly with people, but will occasionally be in a wetlands environment, potentially exposed to weather/wildlife. Work will involve interacting with the public, providing info, standing/sitting, etc

opportunities.title: Volunteer: Orlando Wetlands Tram Tours or Gardening
opportunities.description: Volunteer: Orlando Wetlands Tram Tours or Gardening Orlando Wetlands 0 similar opportunities Similar Opportunities Description Join the Friends of the Orlando Wetlands volunteer group to support the mission and operations of the Orlando Wetlands. Activities include: Wednesday mornings Maintain wildlife garden and other planting projects in the Wetlands. Fridays, Saturdays, and Sundays all day Conducting guided tours and tram tour logistics: recording golf cart tour data, processing donations, managing parking (if needed), etc. Other Periodically, there are other volunteer opportunities to assist with at outreach events, conduct tours for reserved groups, and more. Time Commitment: Volunteers participate on an ongoing and recurring basis such as weekly or monthly. Shift hours vary, but are typically no less than 4 hours in length. Setting: Most work is conducted outdoors in a wetlands environment where volunteers will be exposed to weather, wildlife, people. Work may involve operating tram carts and using basic gardening tools. Skills: No qualifications required. Benefits: Being a friend of the "Friends of the Orlando Wetlands" group! Opportunities to participate in shaping the direction of our programs and volunteer opportunities. Periodic social and learning events. Training: Training will be provided by staff and experienced volunteers. All new volunteers wanting to lead tours will shadow staff and other volunteers on tours until they are ready to lead on their own. Screeni

opportunities.title: Volunteer Open House for Harry P. Leu Gardens - July 2026
opportunities.description: Volunteer Open House for Harry P. Leu Gardens - July 2026 Harry P. Leu Gardens 0 similar opportunities Similar Opportunities Description Harry P. Leu Gardens Volunteer Open House: July 11th, 10:00am to 11:30am Join a vibrant community of nature lovers, history enthusiasts, and service-minded individuals who help make Harry P. Leu Gardens a beautiful and welcoming place for all. From greeting guests and leading tours to helping in the gardens and supporting special events, our volunteers play a vital role in everything we do. Whether you have a few hours a week or are looking for seasonal opportunities, there's a place for you to grow, give back, and connect. Our Volunteer Open House is the start of your volunteering journey! We will cover all the different types of volunteer opportunities available including horticultural, educational, and event support. After the Open House, you will have the option of which team (or teams!) to join and will be given access to all our specific volunteer opportunities. Schedule: 10:00am - Meet & greet staff and volunteers 10:15am - Official start and introduction 10:30am - Department breakdowns 10:50am - Orland Cares Tutorial 11:00am - Questions We will end with an optional tour of the Gardens! Additional info: Minimum age: 18 Availability Schedule Shifts Opportunity available: 07/11/2026 through 18 Positions available! There are no available shifts for this opportunity. Schedule Shift available! Schedule Shifts available! Show hide schedule

opportunities.title: CERT Volunteers - Community Emergency Response Team
opportunities.description: CERT Volunteers - Community Emergency Response Team Orlando Fire Department 0 similar opportunities Similar Opportunities Description Position: The Community Emergency Response Team (CERT) program educates volunteers about disaster preparedness for the hazards that may occur where they live. CERT trains volunteers in basic disaster response skills, such as: Fire safety Light search and rescue Team organization Disaster medical operations The CERT program offers a consistent, nationwide approach to volunteer training and organization that professional responders can rely on during disaster situations, allowing them to focus on more complex tasks. Qualifications: Volunteers must complete a mandatory CERT Basic Training either online or in-person in order to be CERT certified. Volunteers will also need to complete and pass a background screening prior to beginning their service. Responsibilities: Once CERT trained, volunteers will have the opportunity to participate in community events focused on safety and disaster preparedness. Additional info: Minimum age: 16 Availability Schedule Shifts Opportunity available: 06/29/2023 through 205 Positions available! There are no available shifts for this opportunity. Schedule Shift available! Schedule Shifts available! Show hide schedule slots filter schedule shifts Show in Calendar Location Where the opportunity will occur Orlando , FL Location Description: Once CERT Certified, volunteers will join a CERT Team in their District.
